docs.title: Home
docs.text: Welcome to Soflan Notes. This site was created with the goal of sharing IIDX Soflan related information.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The overall goal of this site is to provide information about soflan in IIDX. This includes core concepts, techniques, as well as various examples to help you in the journey of tackling soflan charts.

This site is not meant to be used as a "directory" for individual Soflan charts.

The reason being is that I want people to understand why these techniques exist, rather than copy and pasting a simple strategy. The reason being is that, just like how everyone uses different Green Number and White Number, each should discover a soflan strategy that suits them. By fully understanding how different techniques work and can be utilized, players should be able to explore and find something that is catered to their playstyle, rather than someone else's.

While there will be examples, these are used as an explanation on how one can approach a song rather than a definitive answer.

If there is enough interest, I will create video examples of songs.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
The notes will be divided into different categories:

  • Terminology - The terminologies that will be used throughout the site.
  • Beginner - Covering the core Hi-Speed concepts.
  • Normal - The fundementals techniques that you absolutely need, regardless of tacking easy or hard song.
  • Hyper - Theory crafting on fundementals listed in Basic. This will include some advanced concepts such as pre-floating (theoretical ideas on how one can utilize the techniques mentioned on Basic).
  • Another - Advanced theory crafting + new set of techniques that were discovered post IIDX Bistrover.
  • Leggendaria - Small set of examples going over the concepts covered + few personal notes (i.e. how I tackle new or complex charts).

docs.title: Questions/Comments
docs.text: Please reach out to @_x_ela for any questions or comments about content on this site.

docs.title: Why have a new website for notes?
docs.text:
While there are plenty of information out there on various websites (as well as youtube videos on individual songs), there were couple of things that I did not like.

First, any information post IIDX Bistrover (BPL Season 1) seems to be either uncovered or missing in documentations. This was a time period when, due to the physical changes of where start button is, various new strategies were being experimented on. There were two key important soflan techniques that became a main stay, but I have never seen this being discussed elsewhere.

Second, the uncovered information above is spread out through various videos. While there are few youtube videos that shows what they do for each song, they do not discuss in detail on how/why the technique works.

The overall goal of this website is to share as much information as possible on techniques and theories. Using this information, I want players to apply the techniques and experiment to find what is best 'for them'. But...

docs.title: Why not just post examples to copy from?
docs.text:
I believe that, for the most part, there are various ways to tackle a song. There might be the most 'optimal' way that may seem great for some, but ridiculous to execute for others.

I've noticed over the years that best soflan players find what is best for their playstyle (whether it be physical style or reading style).

docs.title: But...... why have all these complicated explanations? Why not just float at every speed change?
docs.text:
Great point, and I can't really disagree with that. IIDX is already frustrating enough as is, and learning additional stuff just to tackle soflan is a chore.

The reason why... If there is a way to float through a song, I wanted to see if there is a way to minimize the EX points lost from doing the floating. I wanted to see if there is a way to float so that I do not drop any combo. I always wanted to explore the 'what if' to take myself to the next level with soflan.

I understand that most players will neglect information posted here, and that's fine. I'm here for the small percentage of players who may find this information interesting, and trying to help them in their soflan journey.

docs.title: Who the f- plays these songs anyways?
docs.text: I do.

docs.title: Terminologies
docs.text:
List of all terminologies used across the site.

Most of these terminologies should be familiar, and will be linking to external documentation for explanations. For terminologies that have stars (★), these may have slight different meaning and/or specific to this website that is not well defined elsewhere.

Note: This may not align with general terms used by majority of players. I will try my best to align with others as site develops.

Please refer to the basic definitions over from IIDX.org Beginner section, as this page will not cover the concepts (such as how to set Green Number/White Number, turning on FHS and switching between Normal/Classic Hi-Speed).

docs.title: Normal Hi-Speed
docs.text: The default Hi-Speed option that was introduced in IIDX Rootage. Uses value from 1-20 that has preset of Green Numbers.

docs.title: Classic Hi-Speed
docs.text:
The old Hi-Speed option that was implemented since IIDX TRICORO. Uses values from 1.00 to 4.00, with increments of 0.5 from 1.00 to 2.00, then increments of 0.25 from 2.00 to 4.00.

While it is mostly recommended to use Floating Hi-Speed in most cases, it is very important to understand how Classic Hi-Speed (and Hi-Speed Value) works.

docs.title: Floating Hi-Speed (FHS)
docs.text:
The Hi-Speed option that (MOSTLY*) locks in your Green Number, regardless of your White Number.

On this site, it is implied that SUDDEN+ and/or LIFT is used with this option. The FHS without both SUDDEN+/LIFT behaves differently, and is not covered in this site.

*: The mostly is mentioned here because if the song becomes too slow or too fast, the float may not set the GN to your desired number. This is discussed indepth in Another section.

docs.title: Hi-Speed Value
docs.text: The numerical value displayed in the Hi-Speed section of the gear. If Classic Hi-Speed is being used and floating Hi-Speed is off, this would be the Classic Hi-Speed value.

docs.title: Notes Time / Green Number (GN)
docs.text:
The amount of time the notes are visible on screen. GN / (60 * 10) = amount of time, in seconds.

i.e. 300GN = 0.5 seconds.

docs.title: Sub-Length / White Number (WN)
docs.text: The amount of area the SUDDEN+/LIFT covers. The total amount is up to 1000.

docs.title: SUDDEN+
docs.text: The lane cover that appears at the top. Has its own WN to go along with.

docs.title: Lift
docs.text: The option to lift/bring up the judge line. Has its own WN to go along with.

docs.title: Floating (Gear Change)
docs.text: The action of holding down the start button and turning the turntable.

docs.title: X buttons UP/DOWN ★
docs.text:
This refers to holding down start and hitting the black keys (for UP) or the white keys (for DOWN) exactly X times.

Each black key (UP) will add 0.5, and each white key (DOWN) will subtract 0.5 from Hi-Speed Value (in general).

docs.title: Start x4 ★
docs.text: Hitting the Start button 4 times in rapid manner (disables and enables Sudden+)

docs.title: x0.5 Reset ★
docs.text: Act of resetting the Hi-Speed to any multiple of x0.5 while floating Hi-Speed is on, regardless of current WN.

docs.title: FHS On/Off ★
docs.text: Turning on/off FHS by pressing Start + Effect.

docs.title: Hi-Speed (Basics)
docs.text:
This page is for brief introduction to differnet Hi-Speed modes. Please understand that I will not go into indepth details of how each Hi-Speeds work.

If you are not familiar with different Hi-Speed modes and how to use them, please head over to IIDX.org and review [Hi-Speed Options(https://iidx.org/beginner/hispeed) and Floating Hi-Speed

For specific terminologies used on this page, refer to Terminology section.

docs.title: Different Hi-Speed modes
docs.text:
Currently, IIDX has 3 different Hi-Speed modes that you can choose from: - Normal Hi-Speed - Classic Hi-Speed - Floating Hi-Speed

During song select, you can select the option between Normal Hi-Speed and Classic Hi-Speed. You can turn on floating in-game by holding down START + pressing EFFECT.

So the two ways the Hi-Speeds will work are: - Normal Hi-Speed ↔ Floating Hi-Speed - Classic Hi-Speed ↔ Floating Hi-Speed

As mentioned in the terminology section, the Floating Hi-Speed (FHS) works differently when the SUDDEN+/LIFT is on or not.

If you do not use any of the lane cover options, the Green Number (GN) is not locked in. Thus, floating (act of holding down Start then turning the turntable) will cause the GN to change. (To do: Check to see if the behavior is same in terms of still being able to "lock-in" GN).

If you use any of the lane cover options, FHS will lock in your GN. What this means is that, the game will save your GN once the FHS is turned on. Any floats when lane cover will set your GN to the 'saved' value.

It is highly recommended, at the very lease, to use SUDDEN+ even if it only covers only little part of the screen. This is because there's more advantages of having SUDDEN+ on for setting GN as well as floating through soflan songs.

docs.title: FHS usage is (99%) Mandatory due to Floating
docs.text:
As you can see on IIDX.org and various other sites, it is almost mandatory (highly, HIGHLY recommended) to use FHS. Soflan charts is one of the reasons.

As mentioned above, FHS will remember the 'GN' you save when you turn on. The advantage of this is that you can float after a BPM change and it will 'reset' your GN.

For example, lets say a song slowed down going from 200 BPM to 100 BPM. This will double your GN in-game, and the notes will stay on the screen longer than usual (with narrower note spacing). If you float during this BPM change, the game will 'reset' the GN to the saved value, thus reverting back to your normal GN.

Floating (and the ideas behind the usage) will be covered more indepth in Floating (Beginner) and Floating (Advanced).

docs.title: Normal Hi-Speed or Classic Hi-Speed
docs.text:
One question that you may have is this: Is there a difference between these two different Hi-Speed modes?

The answer is bit complex. For those who are just starting out or do not want to go in-depth into soflan journey, I would say there is no difference. The reason why is because either one of these modes are there just to make it easier for you to set your GN before turning the Floating Hi-Speed on.

But if you are interested in learning about the difference, I'll briefly explain my thoughts on both modes. Usage of each mode will be discussed more in-depth in subsequent sections.

docs.title: Classic Hi-Speed
docs.text: There's actually few reasons to use Classic Hi-Speed over Normal Hi-Speed: - Old strategies derive from plyaers using Classic Hi-Speed, which are still viable today if you prefer that style - Allows you to easily set your GN to x1/2 or x2 value before turning on the FHS, without calculating the GN itself. This also applies if song speeds up or slows down by a constant number; it'll make calculations very easy (i.e. If a song goes from 120 BPM to 180 BPM, going from 3.00 Hi-Speed to 2.00 Hi-Speed will keep the GN the same).

docs.title: Normal Hi-Speed
docs.text: Throughout my experimentation, there isn't much advantages of using Normal Hi-Speed over Classic Hi-Speed, except for maybe one reason: - Allows you to set to your desired GN number in almost any situation, as long as you know which GN you want to use.

docs.title: Hi-Speed Value
docs.text: This section is for the Hi-Speed value that is displayed in the gear itself. For the sake of rest of the material on this site, it is HIGHLY recommended (if not mandatory) to have a basic grasp of what this number is.

docs.text:
The Hi-Speed option that (MOSTLY*) locks in your Green Number, regardless of your White Number.

*: The mostly is mentioned here because if the song becomes too slow or too fast, the float may not set the GN to your desired number. This is discussed indepth in Another section.

docs.text: The numerical value displayed in the Hi-Speed section of the gear. If Classic Hi-Speed is being used and floating Hi-Speed is off, this would be the Classic Hi-Speed value.

docs.title: Notes Time / Green Number (GN)
docs.text:
The amount of time the notes are visible on screen. GN / (60 * 10) = amount of time, in seconds.

i.e. 300GN = 0.5 seconds.

docs.title: Sub-Length / White Number (WN)
docs.text: The amount of area the SUDDEN+/LIFT covers. The total amount is up to 1000.

docs.title: SUDDEN+
docs.text: The lane cover that appears at the top. Has its own WN to go along with.

docs.title: Lift
docs.text: The option to lift/bring up the judge line. Has its own WN to go along with.

docs.title: Floating (Gear Change)
docs.text: The action of holding down the start button and turning the turntable.

docs.title: X buttons UP/DOWN ★
docs.text:
This refers to holding down start and hitting the black keys (for UP) or the white keys (for DOWN) exactly X times.

Each black key (UP) will add 0.5, and each white key (DOWN) will subtract 0.5 from Hi-Speed Value (in general).

docs.title: Start x4 ★
docs.text: Hitting the Start button 4 times in rapid manner (disables and enables Sudden+)

docs.title: x0.5 Reset ★
docs.text: Act of resetting the Hi-Speed to any multiple of x0.5 while floating Hi-Speed is on, regardless of current WN.

docs.title: FHS On/Off ★
docs.text: Turning on/off FHS by pressing Start + Effect.
